behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (behavior text): "Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed". Slot B (decisive boundary): handoff between PR-change discovery and analyzer file-selection modules. Slot C (miss if lowered): a Unit test of only the filter function can pass while integration wiring still sends the full repository file list to the scanner. Final label: Integration, because the key risk is Slot B module handoff and Unit would miss Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real change-list provider module (fed with a PR diff fixture) to the real analysis orchestrator and scanner invocation layer; assert scanner receives exactly changed-file paths and zero unchanged-file paths.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The assertion depends on cross-module wiring, not just one function.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (behavior text): "grouped by severity ... in the posted PR comment". Slot B (decisive boundary): handoff from findings model -> grouping/formatting -> PR comment payload builder. Slot C (miss if lowered): a Unit test of formatter logic can pass while adapter code drops a severity bucket when building the outbound comment body. Rejection gate for System: behavior text does not require validating live GitHub API transport, only correctness of grouped content in the produced comment. Final label: Integration, driven by Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run analyzer-output objects through real grouping and comment-construction modules with a fake PR client; assert generated comment contains distinct error/warning/info sections with the correct findings mapped to each.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test scope is module composition and payload correctness, not external API behavior.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (behavior text): "touches 200 files" and "within the 10-minute Actions timeout". Slot B (decisive boundary): real-infra/runtime boundary (process execution time, filesystem I/O, CI-like resource constraints). Slot C (miss if lowered): an Integration test with mocked timers or stubbed linter work can pass while real subprocess/file I/O exceeds timeout in CI. Rejection gate for higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-step agentic pipeline described. Final label: System, because Slot B real runtime behavior is essential and Integration misses Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step end-to-end in a CI-like environment against a generated 200-file PR fixture, measure wall-clock runtime, and fail if completion exceeds 10 minutes.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Only a real execution environment can expose timeout regressions.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (behavior text): "linter process crashes on a malformed file" and "remaining files are still analyzed". Slot B (decisive boundary): real-infra subprocess failure handling (crash/exit semantics, continuation logic). Slot C (miss if lowered): Integration tests that simulate an exception in-process can miss real child-process crash behavior (exit codes, broken pipes, partial output) that prevents continuation/posting. Rejection gate for higher levels: no model output quality or multi-agent sequence involved. Final label: System, based on Slot B and the concrete miss in Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual lint subprocess over a file set containing one crash-triggering malformed file and many valid files; verify orchestrator records the crash, continues with remaining files, and posts aggregated results.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Behavior explicitly targets real process-crash semantics.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Slot A (behavior text): "from overlapping rules" and "deduplicated in the output". Slot B (decisive boundary): handoff/normalization between multiple rule outputs and final dedup/output assembly. Slot C (miss if lowered): a Unit test on a normalized dedup helper can pass while upstream modules emit slightly different path/line encodings, causing duplicates to leak in final output. Rejection gate for System: behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess to trigger; local module wiring with representative rule outputs is sufficient. Final label: Integration, because dedup correctness depends on cross-module data shape consistency (Slot B), which Unit can miss (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: Feed real parsed outputs from at least two overlapping rule modules through the aggregation pipeline and output formatter; assert one final finding per file+line+message identity in emitted comment/report.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This requires cross-module data-shape interaction, not infrastructure.
